posts.caption:
YouTube just tightened the rules on AI-generated and repetitive content. The updated policy spells out which types of videos — mass-produced, low-effort, or inauthentic — are no longer eligible for ad revenue. It's more nuance added to existing rules, not a total crackdown, but creators leaning on AI content farms should pay attention.

Full story + today's brief: link in bio.

#YouTube #CreatorEconomy #AIContent #ContentMonetization #AIPolicy

story.title: YouTube clarifies monetization policy for AI-generated and low-quality content
story.dek: YouTube updated policies defining which AI-generated and repetitive videos can't earn ad revenue, adding nuance to existing inauthentic-content rules.

posts.caption:
Point an iPhone at a screenplay and walk onto the set — virtually. Lightcraft's Spark Story, revealed at SIGGRAPH 2026, converts scripts directly into USD and Gaussian Splat environments you can scout and storyboard on-device before a single light is rigged.

Full story + today's brief: link in bio

#AIFilmmaking #Lightcraft #SIGGRAPH2026 #GenerativeAI #VirtualProduction #CreativeAI

story.title: Lightcraft's Spark Story uses iPhone to generate virtual film sets from scripts
story.dek: SIGGRAPH 2026 revealed Spark Story, which converts screenplays into USD/Gaussian Splat environments and enables on-device shot planning before production.

posts.caption:
Google DeepMind reconstructed Pelé's 1959 'greatest goal' — one that was never filmed. Using AI motion synthesis and Pelé's own account of the play, they built a moving visual record of something that existed only in memory.

Full story + today's brief: link in bio.

#DeepMind #AIVideo #MotionSynthesis #GoogleAI #Pelé #AICreators

story.title: DeepMind AI reconstructs Pelé's legendary unfilmed goal using motion synthesis
story.dek: Google DeepMind recreated a famous 1959 goal Pelé claimed was his best but was never captured on video, using AI to synthesize the play from his account.

posts.caption:
China's AI race is getting serious. Moonshot and Alibaba both dropped new frontier models this week, each claiming performance on par with OpenAI and Anthropic — at a fraction of the cost. The gap between US and Chinese AI labs just got a lot harder to measure.

Full story + today's 6-item brief: link in bio

#ArtificialIntelligence #AINews #Alibaba #MoonshotAI #FrontierAI #AIRace

story.title: Moonshot and Alibaba release frontier AI models challenging OpenAI and Anthropic
story.dek: Chinese AI companies launched competitive models claimed to match OpenAI/Anthropic capabilities at lower cost, narrowing the US AI lead.

posts.caption:
The music industry spent 30 years building legal guardrails around sampling — a few bars, cleared and licensed. AI training datasets swallowed 40 million complete recordings and classified the whole thing as research.

The legal framework simply wasn't built for this scale, and now the industry is scrambling to retrofit century-old copyright logic onto a problem that ate the entire catalog.

Full story + today's brief: link in bio.

#AIMusic #CopyrightLaw #MusicIndustry #GenerativeAI #AIPolicy

story.title: The Sample Clearance Problem, Except It's the Whole Song Now
story.dek: The music industry spent three decades building a legal framework to stop people from lifting four bars of someone else's record. AI just swallowed 40 million complete recordings and called it research.
